articles.title: Sophia and Kepler to marry orbital compute with optical links
articles.summary:
COLORADO SPRINGS – Sophia Space will begin deploying edge compute nodes on Kepler Communications satellites in late 2026, under a strategic pact announced April 13. Through the partnership, Sophia Space will demonstrate its Orbital Data Center (ODC) software, while relying on Kepler’s optical data relay network “to enable distributed, resilient compute infrastructure in space,” according […]
The post Sophia and Kepler to marry orbital compute with optical links appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.summary:

articles.title: Falcon 9 launches Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the ISS
articles.summary:
A Falcon 9 launched a Cygnus cargo spacecraft April 11 as Northrop Grumman continues its dependence on a competitor to fly resupply missions to the International Space Station.
The post Falcon 9 launches Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the ISS appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: La NASA da la bienvenida a la Tierra a los exploradores lunares de Artemis II, quienes batieron récords
articles.summary: Los primeros astronautas en viajar a la Luna en más de medio siglo han regresado a la Tierra tras una misión que ha establecido nuevos récords a bordo del vuelo de prueba Artemis II de la NASA. articles.title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch Aboard Northrop Grumman CRS-24
articles.summary: NASA is sending more science, technology demonstrations, and crew supplies to the International Space Station following the successful launch of the agency’s Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission, or Northrop Grumman CRS-24. Northrop Grumman’s Cygnus XL spacecraft, carrying approximately 11,000 pounds of cargo to the orbiting laboratory, lifted off at 7:41 a.m. EDT Saturday […]

articles.title: Artemis II Splashes Down
articles.summary: This image from April 10, 2026, captures NASA’s Orion spacecraft, with its parachutes deployed, seconds before splashdown in the Pacific Ocean. The Artemis II crew accomplished many milestones on their nearly 10-day mission, surpassing the Apollo 13 record for farthest crewed spaceflight and capturing views of the far side of the Moon.
